text:
First, in the quiet, open lobby with its pale colors and sharp geometry, I feel a certain anticipation mixed with isolation. Spaces like these suggest potential—gatherings, presentations, maybe waiting for something to begin. Yet in their emptiness, I am acutely aware of my own presence, exposed and solitary.

Moving into the second space—the vibrant, text-filled wall of the R+D exhibit—I sense invitation and curiosity. Here, the world feels more alive. The bold colors and words promise ideas, experiments, and the traces of other minds. I feel drawn in, compelled to read and ponder, energized by the manifestation of creativity and inquiry. In this moment, I am present not only in body but also in thought, eager to engage.
